questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Watch for deer crossing the road
Never drive in this lane
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Come to a complete stop
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Come to a complete stop
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Come to a complete stop
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
U-turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Left turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Right turns are not allowed
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Right turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Left turns are not allowed
U-turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Right turns are not allowed
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: U-turns are not allowed
Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Right turns are not allowed
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
U-turns are not allowed
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Start of a no-passing zone
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Start of a no-passing zone
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
An intersection is ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for pedestrians crossing
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
A railroad crossing is ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A series of curves ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
Never drive in this lane
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
An intersection is ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
An intersection is ahead
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A roundabout (circular intersection) is ahead
Sharp right then left turns ahead
Never drive in this lane
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: An intersection is ahead
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for pedestrians crossing
Never drive in this lane
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
A series of curves ahead
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing — yield to trains
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Never drive in this lane
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
A railroad crossing is ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Road construction or detour ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Speed is automatically photo-enforced here
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Road construction or detour ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop and remain stopped until the light turns green
Yield to traffic on the left only
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to come to a complete stop and stay stopped until the signal turns green.

questions.prompt: What shape is a stop sign?
choices: Octagon (eight sides)
Triangle
Diamond
Round circle
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red eight-sided octagon, a shape used for no other sign.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic signal means you should:
choices: Stop, then proceed when it is safe, as at a stop sign
Drive through without stopping
Stop only if other cars are present
Turn around and find another route
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated exactly like a stop sign: stop fully, then go when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: The light is about to turn red, so stop if you can do so safely
Speed up to beat the red light
The light is about to turn green
Stop and wait for a green arrow
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that red is coming, so you should stop when it is safe to do so.

questions.prompt: What color and shape identifies most warning signs that alert you to hazards ahead?
choices: Yellow diamond
Red octagon
Green rectangle
Blue square
questions.explanation: Most warning signs are yellow and diamond-shaped to alert drivers to hazards or changes ahead.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center line means:
choices: Passing is not allowed on your side
You may pass whenever you wish
The road ends ahead
Two-way traffic merges
questions.explanation: A solid yellow center line on your side prohibits passing because oncoming sight distance is limited.

questions.prompt: What does an upside-down triangle sign mean?
choices: Yield the right-of-way
Stop completely
No entry
School crossing
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, telling you to slow down and give way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: Orange traffic signs and cones generally indicate:
choices: A construction or work zone
A hospital nearby
A scenic route
A no-parking area
questions.explanation: Orange signs and devices mark construction and maintenance work zones where extra caution is needed.

questions.prompt: When a police officer is directing traffic at an intersection with working signals, you should:
choices: Obey the officer's directions, even if they differ from the signal
Follow the traffic signal instead of the officer
Stop and wait for the signal to change
Choose whichever you prefer
questions.explanation: Directions from a police officer always override traffic signals and signs.

questions.prompt: White lane lines on a roadway separate:
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
Traffic moving in opposite directions
The road from the shoulder only
Bicycle lanes from sidewalks only
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes that flow in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with no signs or signals, if two vehicles arrive at about the same time, who must yield?
choices: The driver on the left yields to the driver on the right
The faster driver
The driver on the right yields to the driver on the left
Whoever honks first goes
questions.explanation: When vehicles reach an uncontrolled intersection together, you yield to the vehicle on your right.

questions.prompt: When you are turning left at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: Oncoming traffic and crossing pedestrians
Only vehicles behind you
No one if you have a green light
Only large trucks
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing the street being entered.

questions.prompt: When an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on, you should:
choices: Pull over to the right edge of the road and stop
Speed up to get out of the way
Stop immediately in your lane
Continue at the same speed
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, which vehicle has the right-of-way?
choices: The vehicle that arrived and stopped first
The largest vehicle
The vehicle on the left
The vehicle going straight only
questions.explanation: At a four-way stop the first vehicle to stop goes first; ties are resolved by yielding to the right.

questions.prompt: A pedestrian is crossing in a crosswalk ahead of you. You should:
choices: Stop and let the pedestrian cross safely
Honk so they hurry
Drive around them
Proceed because vehicles always have priority
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield to pedestrians in marked or unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: Before making a turn, you should signal:
choices: Well in advance so others know your intention
Only after you start turning
Only at night
Just as you complete the turn
questions.explanation: Signaling early gives other drivers and pedestrians time to react to your turn.

questions.prompt: When entering a road from a private driveway, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic and pedestrians already on the road
Take the right-of-way immediately
Sound your horn and proceed
Stop only if a car is touching you
questions.explanation: A vehicle entering from a driveway must yield to traffic and pedestrians already using the roadway.

questions.prompt: Having the right-of-way at an intersection means:
choices: You should still yield if needed to avoid a crash
You never have to slow down
Other drivers must always stop for you
You can ignore pedestrians
questions.explanation: The law says who must yield, not who may force the way; always give it up to prevent a collision.

questions.prompt: When making a right turn, you should generally turn from:
choices: The lane closest to the right curb into the nearest lane
The center lane
Any lane that is open
The left lane to swing wide
questions.explanation: Right turns are made from the right lane into the lane nearest the right edge of the road being entered.

questions.prompt: When two vehicles meet on a narrow mountain road where neither can pass, who has the right-of-way?
choices: The vehicle heading uphill
The vehicle heading downhill
The larger vehicle
Whoever arrives last
questions.explanation: On a steep narrow road the vehicle going downhill should yield and back up, since it has more control reversing.

questions.prompt: In South Dakota, what is the speed limit in an urban district unless otherwise posted?
choices: 25 mph
15 mph
45 mph
55 mph
questions.explanation: The default speed limit in an urban district in South Dakota is 25 mph unless signs say otherwise.

questions.prompt: What is the maximum speed limit on rural interstate highways in South Dakota?
choices: 80 mph
65 mph
75 mph
70 mph
questions.explanation: South Dakota allows a maximum of 80 mph on rural interstate highways.

questions.prompt: The three-second rule helps you:
choices: Maintain a safe following distance
Decide when to change oil
Time how long to signal
Measure your stopping distance in feet
questions.explanation: The three-second rule sets a minimum following gap so you have time to react to the car ahead.

questions.prompt: Even when the posted limit is higher, you must:
choices: Drive at a speed that is safe for current conditions
Always drive the posted maximum
Drive at least 10 mph over to keep up
Match the speed of the slowest car
questions.explanation: The basic speed law requires a reasonable and prudent speed for weather, traffic, and road conditions.

questions.prompt: You should increase your following distance when:
choices: The road is wet, icy, or visibility is poor
Traffic is light and dry
You are in a hurry
You are driving downhill on a clear day
questions.explanation: Slippery or low-visibility conditions increase stopping distance, so you need more space ahead.

questions.prompt: When following a large truck, you should:
choices: Leave extra following distance
Follow closely to draft
Stay in its blind spot
Pass on the right shoulder
questions.explanation: Trucks block your view and need more room, so a larger following gap keeps you safe.

questions.prompt: Speeding through a posted school zone is dangerous because:
choices: Children may be present and reduced limits apply
The road is always icy there
Police never patrol there
Cars get better mileage when slow
questions.explanation: School zones have lower limits because children may unexpectedly enter the roadway.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic without reason can be dangerous because it:
choices: Blocks traffic and can cause crashes
Saves no fuel
Is always legal and safe
Improves visibility for others
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly impedes traffic flow and can lead to unsafe passing and rear-end crashes.

questions.prompt: The best way to handle a sharp curve is to:
choices: Slow down before entering the curve
Brake hard in the middle of the curve
Accelerate into the curve
Coast in neutral
questions.explanation: Reducing speed before the curve keeps your tires gripping and avoids skidding mid-turn.

questions.prompt: Leaving a space cushion around your vehicle helps you:
choices: Have room to react and escape hazards
Drive faster legally
Avoid using turn signals
Park more easily
questions.explanation: Space on all sides gives you time and an escape path when traffic suddenly changes.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow center line means:
choices: You may pass when it is safe to do so
Passing is never allowed
The road is one-way
You must change lanes
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line allows passing from your side when the way ahead is clear.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, the left lane is intended mainly for:
choices: Passing slower vehicles
Driving slowly to enjoy the view
Parking during emergencies
Bicycles only
questions.explanation: Keep right except to pass; the left lane is for overtaking, not for cruising slowly.

questions.prompt: You should not pass another vehicle when:
choices: Approaching a hill or curve where you cannot see far ahead
The road is straight and clear
A broken yellow line is on your side
Traffic ahead is far away
questions.explanation: Limited sight distance on hills and curves makes passing dangerous and usually illegal.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to the right lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
Your front bumper clears it
You are exactly even with it
Immediately, regardless of distance
questions.explanation: Seeing the whole vehicle in your mirror confirms there is enough room to merge back safely.

questions.prompt: While being passed by another vehicle, you should:
choices: Maintain your speed or slow slightly
Speed up to prevent the pass
Move toward the center line
Brake hard
questions.explanation: Keeping or reducing your speed lets the other driver complete the pass quickly and safely.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal and check mirrors and blind spots
Just look in the rearview mirror
Honk and move over
Close your eyes and steer
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking blind spots prevents collisions with vehicles you cannot see in mirrors alone.

questions.prompt: Passing is generally permitted on the right only when:
choices: A vehicle ahead is turning left and there is room
You are in a hurry
The road is two lanes total
It is nighttime
questions.explanation: Passing on the right is allowed in limited cases, such as when the vehicle ahead is turning left.

questions.prompt: When you cannot pass safely, the best choice is to:
choices: Be patient and wait for a safe opportunity
Tailgate to pressure the driver ahead
Pass on the shoulder
Flash your high beams repeatedly
questions.explanation: Forcing a pass is dangerous; waiting for a clear, legal stretch is far safer.

questions.prompt: A solid white lane line means you should:
choices: Stay in your lane and avoid changing lanes
Pass freely
Stop ahead
Merge left
questions.explanation: A solid white line discourages lane changes between lanes going the same direction.

questions.prompt: Under South Dakota's Move Over law, when passing a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights on an interstate, you should:
choices: Move into the lane farthest from the stopped vehicle if safe
Speed up to pass quickly
Stop in your lane
Sound your horn and continue
questions.explanation: On an interstate you must move over to the lane farthest from a stopped vehicle showing flashing lights.

questions.prompt: Under the Move Over law on a two-lane highway where you cannot change lanes, you must:
choices: Slow to at least 20 mph below the posted speed limit
Keep your current speed
Stop completely
Move onto the shoulder
questions.explanation: On a two-lane road you must slow to at least 20 mph under the posted limit when passing a stopped vehicle with flashing lights.

questions.prompt: Because motorcycles are harder to see, drivers should:
choices: Look twice before turning or changing lanes
Share a lane beside them
Follow closely
Assume they will move out of the way
questions.explanation: Motorcycles are small and easy to miss, so an extra look helps prevent turning into one.

questions.prompt: When a large truck makes a wide right turn, you should:
choices: Stay back and not try to pass between the truck and the curb
Squeeze through on the right
Honk and pass on the left
Stop in the intersection
questions.explanation: Trucks swing wide to turn, and squeezing alongside puts you in a deadly blind spot.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Give them room and pass only when safe
Pass closely to save time
Expect them to ride on the sidewalk
Honk to make them move
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have a right to the road, so give them plenty of space when passing.

questions.prompt: A slow-moving vehicle, such as farm equipment, is marked with:
choices: An orange triangle emblem
A blue circle
A green square
A red octagon
questions.explanation: An orange reflective triangle warns that a vehicle travels much slower than normal traffic.

questions.prompt: The large blind spots around a truck or bus where the driver cannot see you are called:
choices: No-zones
Comfort zones
Crumple zones
Safe lanes
questions.explanation: No-zones are the front, rear, and side blind spots of large vehicles where you should avoid lingering.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian who appears to be blind using a white cane, you should:
choices: Yield and give them the right-of-way
Honk to alert them
Drive around quickly
Expect them to wait for you
questions.explanation: Pedestrians using a white cane or guide dog must always be given the right-of-way.

questions.prompt: When following a motorcycle, you should allow:
choices: Extra following distance because they can stop quickly
Less distance since they are small
The same distance as a truck only
No distance if traffic is slow
questions.explanation: Motorcycles can stop faster than cars, so extra space prevents rear-end collisions.

questions.prompt: In South Dakota, a driver age 21 or older is legally impaired at a blood alcohol concentration (BAC) of:
choices: 0.08 percent or higher
0.10 percent or higher
0.05 percent or higher
0.15 percent or higher
questions.explanation: It is illegal to drive with a BAC of 0.08 percent or more if you are 21 or older.

questions.prompt: Under South Dakota's zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 may not drive with a BAC of:
choices: 0.02 percent or more
0.08 percent or more
0.05 percent or more
0.10 percent or more
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 face a zero-tolerance limit of 0.02 percent because the legal drinking age is 21.

questions.prompt: The only thing that will reliably reduce your blood alcohol concentration is:
choices: Time
Coffee
A cold shower
Exercise
questions.explanation: Only time lets the body process alcohol; coffee and other tricks do not speed it up.

questions.prompt: In South Dakota, instruction permit holders may use a wireless communication device while driving:
choices: Never
Only on highways
Only at night
Only when stopped at lights
questions.explanation: Permit holders are prohibited from using any wireless communication device while driving.

questions.prompt: Texting while driving in South Dakota is:
choices: Prohibited statewide
Allowed below 25 mph
Allowed on rural roads
Allowed for adults only
questions.explanation: South Dakota law bans texting while driving for all drivers statewide.

questions.prompt: Alcohol affects your driving by:
choices: Slowing reaction time and impairing judgment
Improving your reflexes
Sharpening your vision
Making you a safer driver
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reactions, weakens judgment, and reduces coordination, all of which impair driving.

questions.prompt: Which of the following can impair your ability to drive safely?
choices: Some prescription and over-the-counter medications
Drinking water
Listening to a passenger
Wearing sunglasses in daylight
questions.explanation: Many legal medications cause drowsiness or slowed reactions, so check labels before driving.

questions.prompt: To avoid distraction while driving, you should:
choices: Set up navigation and music before you start driving
Read texts at red lights
Eat a full meal while driving
Take notes as you drive
questions.explanation: Handling distractions before you move keeps your attention on the road while driving.

questions.prompt: The safest way to avoid impaired driving is to:
choices: Plan a sober ride before drinking
Drive slowly after drinking
Drink only beer
Wait 15 minutes after your last drink
questions.explanation: Arranging a designated driver or other sober ride in advance keeps everyone safe.

questions.prompt: In South Dakota, you must turn on your headlights:
choices: Whenever you use your windshield wipers due to rain, snow, or fog
Only after dark
Only on the interstate
Only when other cars approach
questions.explanation: South Dakota law requires headlights on any time wipers are needed for weather, improving visibility.

questions.prompt: In fog, you should use:
choices: Low-beam headlights
High-beam headlights
No headlights
Hazard lights while moving at full speed
questions.explanation: Low beams reduce glare in fog, while high beams reflect back and worsen visibility.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer where you want to go
Brake hard and hold the wheel still
Accelerate to regain control
Close your eyes and brace
questions.explanation: Easing off the accelerator and steering toward your intended path helps recover from a skid.

questions.prompt: In winter, which road surfaces freeze first?
choices: Bridges and overpasses
Gravel roads only
Hills only
Parking lots only
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses lose heat from above and below, so they ice over before other surfaces.

questions.prompt: When driving on wet or icy roads, you should:
choices: Slow down and brake gently
Brake hard at the last moment
Drive the posted maximum
Follow closely to see better
questions.explanation: Reduced traction means you must lower speed and brake smoothly to avoid skidding.

questions.prompt: If heavy snow or fog makes it impossible to see safely, you should:
choices: Pull completely off the road and turn on hazard lights
Stop in your lane
Speed up to get through it
Turn on high beams and continue
questions.explanation: Getting fully off the roadway with hazards on keeps you out of the path of other traffic.

questions.prompt: Before driving after a snowfall, you should:
choices: Clear snow and ice from windows, mirrors, and lights
Clear only the driver's window
Drive off and let wind clear it
Use only the defroster while moving
questions.explanation: Full visibility and visible lights are essential, so clear all glass and lamps before driving.

questions.prompt: Hydroplaning, when tires ride on a film of water, is most likely when you:
choices: Drive too fast on a wet road
Drive slowly through a puddle
Use your wipers
Drive on dry pavement
questions.explanation: Higher speeds on wet roads can lift the tires onto the water, causing a loss of control.

questions.prompt: When the sun creates glare that makes it hard to see, you should:
choices: Slow down and use your sun visor
Speed up to pass the glare
Close one eye
Turn off your headlights
questions.explanation: Reducing speed and blocking glare with the visor gives you time to react to what you cannot see clearly.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Toward the curb
Away from the curb
Straight ahead
It does not matter
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb lets it stop the car from rolling into traffic if it moves.

questions.prompt: You should never park within:
choices: An intersection or on a crosswalk
A marked parking stall
A driveway you own
A parking garage
questions.explanation: Parking in intersections or crosswalks blocks traffic and endangers pedestrians, and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: When facing uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Away from the curb
Toward the curb
Straight ahead
Toward oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels away from the curb lets it catch the tires if the car rolls backward.

questions.prompt: You may park in a space reserved for persons with disabilities only if:
choices: You have the proper permit or plate
You will be quick
No other spaces are open
It is after business hours
questions.explanation: Accessible spaces require a valid disability permit or plate regardless of how briefly you stop.

questions.prompt: If you must stop because of an emergency on a highway, you should:
choices: Pull as far onto the shoulder as possible and turn on hazard lights
Stop in the driving lane
Park on a curve for visibility
Leave the vehicle in the lane and walk for help
questions.explanation: Getting off the traveled lane with hazards on warns others and keeps you out of traffic.

questions.prompt: Before leaving a parallel parking space, you should:
choices: Signal and check for traffic and bicyclists
Pull out quickly without looking
Honk and go
Back up first without signaling
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking traffic and bike lanes prevents collisions when re-entering the road.

questions.prompt: It is illegal to park your vehicle:
choices: In front of a fire hydrant
In a driveway with permission
In a metered space with payment
In a marked lot stall
questions.explanation: Parking in front of a fire hydrant blocks emergency access and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: Whenever you leave your parked vehicle, you should:
choices: Set the parking brake and remove the key
Leave it running in gear
Leave the doors open
Leave it in neutral on a hill
questions.explanation: Setting the brake and securing the vehicle prevents it from rolling or being stolen.

questions.prompt: On a roadway with no curb, you should not stop or park:
choices: On the traveled part of the road when you can pull off
On a wide shoulder
In a designated rest area
In a parking lot
questions.explanation: Stopping in the travel lane is hazardous; pull off the road whenever it is possible.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for an instruction permit in South Dakota?
questions.explanation: South Dakota allows applicants to apply for an instruction permit at age 14.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the South Dakota written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: The South Dakota knowledge test consists of 25 multiple-choice questions.

questions.prompt: What score do you need to pass the South Dakota written knowledge test?
choices: 80 percent (20 of 25)
70 percent
90 percent
60 percent
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the questions correctly, which is 20 out of 25.

questions.prompt: While driving with an instruction permit between 10:00 p.m. and 6:00 a.m., who must be in the front passenger seat?
choices: A parent or guardian
Any friend over 16
No one is required
A licensed driver of any age
questions.explanation: During the late-night hours a parent or guardian must ride in the front seat with a permit holder.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving practice are required for South Dakota teen drivers progressing through graduated licensing?
choices: 50 hours, including 10 at night and 10 in inclement weather
20 hours total
100 hours total
30 hours, all in daylight
questions.explanation: Teens must log 50 supervised hours with at least 10 at night and 10 in inclement weather.

questions.prompt: An instruction permit holder must be accompanied by a licensed driver who is at least:
choices: 18 years old with one year of driving experience
16 years old
21 years old
Any age with a license
questions.explanation: The supervising driver must be 18 or older with at least one year of driving experience.

questions.prompt: To get an instruction permit, an applicant must first pass:
choices: A vision screening and the knowledge test
Only a driving test
Only a vision screening
A physical fitness test
questions.explanation: Applicants must pass a vision screening and the written knowledge test (unless exempt through driver education).

questions.prompt: If you change your address, you should:
choices: Notify the Driver Licensing Program as required
Do nothing until renewal
Only tell your insurance company
Wait until you are stopped by police
questions.explanation: Keeping your address current with the licensing agency ensures you receive important notices.

questions.prompt: By driving on South Dakota roads, you give implied consent to:
choices: Chemical testing if suspected of impaired driving
Searches of your home
Random vehicle inspections
Waiving your license fee
questions.explanation: Implied consent means you agree to chemical testing when an officer reasonably suspects impairment.

questions.prompt: In South Dakota, seat belts are required for:
choices: The driver and front-seat passengers
The driver only
Rear-seat passengers only
No one, it is optional
questions.explanation: State law requires the driver and front-seat passengers to wear seat belts.

questions.prompt: Refusing a lawful chemical test for alcohol in South Dakota can result in:
choices: License revocation
A warning only
No consequences
A reduced fine
questions.explanation: Refusing the test carries its own license revocation separate from any DUI penalty.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, you are required to:
choices: Stop, help the injured, and exchange information
Leave if no police are present
Drive away if your car still runs
Only stop if the other driver does
questions.explanation: Drivers must stop, aid the injured, exchange information, and report the crash as required by law.

questions.prompt: In South Dakota, children under what age must be secured in an approved child restraint?
choices: Under 5 years old
Under 2 years old
Under 8 years old
Under 10 years old
questions.explanation: Children younger than 5 must be secured in an approved child passenger restraint system.

questions.prompt: Driving in South Dakota is considered:
choices: A privilege that can be suspended or revoked
A guaranteed right
Automatic once you turn 16
Permanent once licensed
questions.explanation: Driving is a privilege, and unsafe or illegal driving can lead to suspension or revocation.

questions.prompt: When a law enforcement officer requests it, you must show your:
choices: Driver license, registration, and proof of insurance
Birth certificate only
School ID
Passport only
questions.explanation: You must carry and present your license, registration, and proof of insurance when asked.

questions.prompt: Accumulating too many traffic violations can result in:
choices: Suspension of your driving privileges
A free driving course
An automatic license upgrade
No effect at all
questions.explanation: Repeated violations can lead to suspension of your license along with fines and higher insurance costs.

questions.prompt: Penalties for impaired driving in South Dakota are stricter for drivers under 21 because:
choices: The state has a zero-tolerance standard for underage drivers
Young drivers pay less for licenses
They drive newer cars
They take longer tests
questions.explanation: Because the drinking age is 21, underage drivers face a strict 0.02 zero-tolerance limit and harsher penalties.
